topic.what: Multi-modal transformer model for software vulnerability detection
topic.how: Integrates function-level code tokens, reduced non-terminal AST nodes, and code stylometry (CStyle) features in a RoBERTa-based architecture with masked language modeling pretraining on 4.9M functions
topic.why_matters: Captures stylistic and structural cues missed by token-only models, enabling 4-48% F1 gains over SOTA baselines and SOTA performance on BigVul and VulDeePecker

characteristics.what_it_measures: Vulnerability detection accuracy (harmonic mean of precision/recall)
characteristics.vs_baseline: +11.23% over CodeBERT, +6.00% over UniXcoder on BigVul
characteristics.evidence: Table IV: BigVul F1=95.06 vs CodeBERT 83.83, UniXcoder 89.06
characteristics.context: Best performing dataset; full model outperforms all single-modality variants by 4-48% F1

characteristics.evidence: Table IV: VulDeePecker F1=97.76 vs CodeBERT 93.20, UniXcoder 93.44
characteristics.context: SOTA performance; CStyle and AST modalities complement token features

characteristics.evidence: Table IV: Devign F1=62.25 vs CodeBERT 56.86, UniXcoder 58.19
characteristics.context: Function-level classification; baseline models perform similarly

characteristics.evidence: Table IV: REVEAL F1=45.27 vs CodeBERT 42.33, UniXcoder 42.79
characteristics.context: Highly imbalanced dataset; weighted loss applied during training

characteristics.what_it_measures: Ratio of true positives to all positive predictions on BigVul
characteristics.evidence: Table IV: BigVul Precision=95.38 vs CodeBERT 88.86

characteristics.what_it_measures: Ratio of correctly detected vulnerabilities on BigVul
characteristics.evidence: Table IV: BigVul Recall=94.73 vs CodeBERT 79.33

characteristics.what_it_measures: F1 improvement from adding AST structure on BigVul
characteristics.context: Non-terminal AST nodes provide semantic hierarchy without full tree overhead

characteristics.what_it_measures: F1 improvement from adding stylometry features on BigVul
characteristics.context: CStyle captures developer habits correlating with vulnerability risk
RoBERTaBASE architecture with 12 encoder layers, 768 hidden size

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of functions used for pretraining
characteristics.vs_baseline: Larger than most code pretraining datasets

good_for: function-level vulnerability detection in C/C++ code
detecting style-correlated vulnerabilities (memory misuse, pointer errors, buffer handling)
CI/CD pipeline integration for pre-commit or merge review
identifying developer habit patterns correlating with risk
not_for: cryptographic misuse or integer overflow without style signal
LLM-generated or auto-formatted code with uniform style
purely semantic flaws not tied to coding behavior
requires: C/C++ code with available ground-truth labels
function-level granularity; does not operate on line-level
